name: Neural Homophily — Similar Neural Responses Predict Friendship
description: fMRI data from 42 first-year graduate students embedded in a fully characterized real-world social network (N=279). Participants free-viewed naturalistic videos while brain responses were recorded, allowing the study of 'neural homophily': friends exhibit exceptionally similar neural responses, and similarity decreases with social distance. A landmark dataset connecting brain activity to social-network structure.
citation: Parkinson C, Kleinbaum AM, Wheatley T (2018). Similar neural responses predict friendship. Nature Communications, 9, 332.

publications.title: Neural similarity predicts whether strangers become friends
